prompt: Review revision REV-07 of accepted plan PLAN-12 after an implementation fix. The prior review found a pre-existing documentation defect and an in-scope failing test. The fix resolves the test but introduces a direct regression in the changed parser. Run a delta recheck and classify the result. Then receive an accepted Scope Delta that expands the contract.

requires: new Contract version after accepted Scope Delta

must: retain PLAN-12 and REV-07 identities while the scope and contract remain unchanged
recheck the fixed claim and directly affected surfaces without inventing unrelated new blockers
allow a new blocker when direct evidence shows a regression introduced by the fix
classify the pre-existing documentation defect as FOLLOW-UP rather than a new acceptance blocker
reject acceptance while any current blocker remains; BLOCKER never implies ACCEPT
create a new Contract version only after the accepted Scope Delta and re-enter planning for that changed scope
must_not: renumber PLAN-12 or REV-07 merely because a local fix or recheck occurred
raise unrelated newly discovered work as a delta-review blocker without a direct fix connection
downgrade a fix-introduced direct regression because it was discovered during a delta recheck
treat a pre-existing defect as a new blocker when it is outside the accepted scope
return ACCEPT when a BLOCKER is present
create a new Contract version for a failed check, new hypothesis, or known local fix alone
evidence: Static review contract for stable identities, bounded delta findings, monotonic acceptance, and scope-authoritative contract versioning.
